description: SRE Core — Compiler and Canonical Source Language Definition

layers.name: Canonical Source
layers.description: The source language of the SRE. Human-authored JSON/YAML documents defining entities, representations, affordances, interactions, relationships, and language mappings.

layers.name: Compiler
layers.description: Validates canonical source and compiles it into an optimized runtime graph. Deterministic: same source → same output hash.

layers.name: Runtime
layers.description: Compiled graph artifacts. Immutable once written. Never edited — recompiled from source.

layers.name: Plugins
layers.description: Optional plugins for embedding, storage, TTS, STT, and lesson strategies. All plugins are swappable.

layers.name: Formal Semantics
layers.description: Mathematical definitions, cardinality rules, inference rules, compiler pipeline spec, and validation rules.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Schema validation, contract checks, golden output comparisons, compiler pipeline tests.

invariants: Reality precedes language. Nothing exists because it has a word.
Meaning is interaction. Definitions are summaries of repeated interactions.
Languages are projections. No language is privileged.
AI reasons over representations, not word-to-word mappings.
The runtime graph is immutable. Never edited — always recompiled.
Compilation is deterministic. Same source → same output hash.
Every compiled artifact carries provenance tracing to its source location.
Embeddings are optional. The compiler works without any embedding plugin.
No architectural changes unless implementation exposes a real limitation.
